event: Christ taking leave of his mother before the Passion
symbols: Christ's extended hand toward the women — his compassionate farewell and blessing before suffering
Mary collapsed in grief — the sword that will pierce her heart, Simeon's prophecy being fulfilled
lush trees contrasted with bare branch — life and death intertwined in this moment of departure
architectural ruin — the old order about to be transformed through the Passion
description: A rich, colorful composition set in an outdoor scene between architecture on the left and lush green trees on the right. On the right side, Christ in a dark red robe extends his hand toward a group of women on the left. Two male figures in robes stand behind Christ. On the left, a group of women cluster around the Virgin Mary, who appears to have collapsed in grief, wearing a dark blue robe. Other women in white head coverings support her. An elaborate architectural structure with columns and a balcony fills the upper left. Mountains and a blue sky with clouds form the background.
theological_description: Christ Taking Leave of His Mother is a devotional subject that dramatizes the moment before Christ sets out on the road to Jerusalem and his Passion. While not narrated as a single scene in the Gospels, the tradition draws on the emotional logic of the Incarnation: the Son who became flesh through Mary must now depart from her to fulfill his redemptive mission. The scene anticipates Simeon's prophecy that a sword would pierce Mary's soul (Luke 2:35). Christ's gesture of farewell combines tenderness with resolve — he goes willingly to the cross, as he declared: 'No one takes my life from me, but I lay it down of my own accord' (John 10:18). Mary's collapse embodies the cost of discipleship for those closest to Jesus. The scene also echoes Christ's words at the wedding at Cana, 'My hour has not yet come' (John 2:4) — now that hour has arrived, and the separation begins. The holy women who support Mary prefigure the same women who will stand at the foot of the cross (John 19:25).

scripture_refs.reason: Simeon's prophecy that a sword would pierce Mary's soul — fulfilled as Christ departs toward the Passion
Christ laying down his life of his own accord — his voluntary departure toward suffering
The women at the cross, prefigured in the women who gather around Mary at the leave-taking
My hour has not yet come — now that hour arrives and the farewell must happen
The Gethsemane agony that follows this departure, Christ accepting the cup of suffering

_meta.title: Christ Taking Leave of His Mother
